commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=15fc108b53749c81aed88e12f06c8cfff7f4718a)
commands.review_summary:
The PR replaces three Feishu `response.json()` reads with 4 MiB `readResponseWithLimit` buffers and updates streaming-card mocks to return real `Response` bodies.

PR surface: Source +25, Tests -6. Total +19 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level reproduction: current main has unbounded `response.json()` reads in the Feishu streaming token and create-card success paths. I did not run a live Feishu API or OOM repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs a small code fix plus contributor real behavior proof; because proof must come from a real setup, this should stay in human review rather than an automated repair lane.

target.title: fix(feishu): bound JSON response reads to prevent OOM
target.body:
## Summary

Bound three unbounded `response.json()` reads in the feishu extension with `readResponseWithLimit` (4 MB cap) to prevent oversized API responses from exhausting process memory.

- `fetchFeishuJson` in `app-registration.ts` — shared helper used by init, begin, poll, and app query flows
- `getToken` in `streaming-card.ts` — tenant access token endpoint
- `start` in `streaming-card.ts` — card creation response

All three call sites already use `fetchWithSsrFGuard` for SSRF protection but lacked response size limits. The bound follows the established pattern from sibling extensions (signal, googlechat, matrix).

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Unbounded `response.json()` reads in feishu extension can exhaust process memory if the API returns an oversized response.

**Environment tested**: Linux x64, Node.js v24.13.1

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/app-registration.test.ts
pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/streaming-card.test.ts
```

**After-fix evidence**:

```
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  2 passed (2)

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  17 passed (17)

 Total: 2 files, 19 tests — all pass
```

**What was tested**: Unit tests for both modified files (19 tests total). No live feishu API test was run — the change is a mechanical replacement of `response.json()` with `readResponseWithLimit` + `JSON.parse`, matching the identical pattern used in signal, googlechat, and matrix extensions.

## Tests and validation

### Unit tests

All 19 tests pass across both test files:
- `extensions/feishu/src/app-registration.test.ts` — 2 tests
- `extensions/feishu/src/streaming-card.test.ts` — 17 tests

### Regression coverage

No behavior change for normal-size responses (< 4 MB). Oversized responses now throw instead of silently consuming unbounded memory. Test mocks were updated to return real `Response` objects where `readResponseWithLimit` reads the body stream.

## Risk checklist

- [x] This change is backwards compatible
- [x] This change has been tested with existing configurations
- [ ] I have updated relevant documentation (N/A — internal change, no new API surface)
- [ ] Breaking changes (if any) are documented in Summary (N/A — additive guard only)

**Merge-risk explanation**: Minimal. Each replacement is mechanical: `response.json()` → `readResponseWithLimit()` + `JSON.parse(new TextDecoder().decode(buffer))`. Three call sites, all internal to the feishu extension. No config, routing, or API contract changes.

## Related

- Inspired by Alix-007's bound response PR pattern (27 merged PRs across extensions)
- Feishu was one of the remaining unbounded extensions

## Current review state

- [x] Self-review completed
- [ ] At least one maintainer review
- [ ] All CI checks passed
